Match_Summary_Notes:
Overall, Georgsson had a good game despite conceding 2 goals. He had a very good sense of positioning during most instances of the game, whether it was in possession or out of possession. The advanced position he had for most of the game would be hard for most keepers to sustain but Georgsson knew exactly where to stand, when to rush out and when to track back.
- In possession: Georgsson looked very composed, always picking the right pass (91% passing accuracy). He has a very good technique, which let him complete 8/13 (62%) of his long balls and 6/11 (55%) of his passes in the opposition half. He correctly identified whom he should pass to and applied the right weight to complete the pass, making it easier for the attacking players to control the ball during his long balls. One particular instance should be mentioned too, his long, ground pass at 61:50. Brilliant idea to play the ball into the attackers, and a good execution, he was just unlucky the defender got there first but this is a path to explore more often when possible as it could create clear opportunities with third man runs off the ball by one of the attacking midfielders or the wingers, even though it is hard to do such things from a position that deep. Playing without being under pressure certainly helped him too, but even when the backline was pressured, he knew exactly where to go in order to be the extra man and offer solutions with his positioning and quick passes. He also wasn't scared from playing the ball into the central areas, and knew when to do so without creating problems for his team. Finally, he knew exactly when to accelerate the tempo and try to launch a counter-attack, and when to slow down the play.

- Out of possesion: Most of the time, Georgsson positioned himself well to claim loose balls from deeper Keflavik players and mitigate any risk. He had very quick reactions to claim crosses and the loose balls in general. His speed and positioning discouraged the opposition from pressing him whenever he wanted to claim a ball. He also made one good save, catching the ball with a diving save to his near post from a shot outside the box after closing the space between him and the near post down well. That being said, his performance wasn't flawless. At 11:40, he concedes an offside goal, while maybe he hadn't warmed up after getting subbed on 1 minute earlier, he still didn't close down the angle well to be able to stop the shot, it looks like Ljubicic (#10) had it very easy to slot the ball past him from such a distance. For the first real goal at 54:13 (1-1), his positioning was good, but maybe he could've closed down the space a bit more in anticipation of the tap-in, in order to have a better chance to save the shot, but these are still very hard to save and overall he still did well. Finally, for the second goal at 78:30 (1-2), he made an error by being far too focused on the ball and not scanning the box to see where Ljubicic is, moreover, his body orientation was also at 100% towards the ball, while this is understandable since he was awaiting the cross, if he were a bit more rotated toward the rest of the box he probably would have been able to save/claim the ball. He therefore was slow to come back and could not make it back in time to stop the ball from going in.
Monthly_Analysis: Georgsson has had a nice start to the season. He showed a very high technical level, being really composed under pressure and helping the team a lot during the in possession phases of the game. In fact, he had a 90.9% passing accuracy this month, an elite figure. He also made improvements in his shot stopping and sweeper actions, being more confident because he got used to it. After being hesitant while rushing out and having a positioning that left something to desire in the first few games, Georgsson improved a lot in this aspect ad pulled off great saves in the later games of the month of April. Even though he did not keep a clean sheet that month and his xG Prevented is at -6.39, he still performed at a high level both on the ball and off of it. He could have done better in some actions especially regarding some goals he conceded, but the high defensive line does make it harder for him as he's left exposed a lot of times.

Match_Summary_Notes:
Even though Georgsson conceded 2 goals against Þór, he still had a decent performance overall. Just like last game, he appeared confident and his positioning helped him stop attacks from the opposition.
- In Possession: Georgsson was very composed on the ball, even when multiple players were pressing him. He calmly assessed the situation and most of the time made a good pass by choosing the correct teammate and applying the right weight which made the receptions very easy for the outfield players. Even though he was a bit less involved in the buildup than last week (9 passes less), he still maintained a very good accuracy percentage, with 89% across both games. He also had some good throws, he knew when and who to throw it to. Even his clearances were good, as he always tried to aim at one of his teammates instead of randomly clearing the ball and risking of losing it.
- Out of Possession: Let's start with the goals. For the first goal, Georgsson made a good decision to rush out but he tried to mostly cover a low shot instead of making himself bigger. Since he focused more on that than trying to prevent a chip too, he made it easier for the opponent to lob him. He should have tried to make himself a bit bigger instead of trying to prevent a ground shot so much. For the second goal, Georgsson was not at fault, there was nearly nothing he could have done to effectively stop the ball but he could have still followed it instead of staying frozen during the cross. This made it far too easy for the opponent to finish into an empty net, while the main goal in these situations is to be as close to the ball as possible and trying to put pressure on the attacker. There was one more thing that needs to be mentioned. During the corner at the 20th minute, Georgsson rushed out to punch the ball but couldn't because one of his teammates tried to clear it with his head, which nearly caused a goal. Georgsson could have communicated with his teammate to leave the ball as he was most certainly getting there, instead of risking it like this. Other than that, Georgsson was pretty good. He claimed many crosses even though he mostly wasn't under pressure and knew how to defend his box during corners, showing once again great composure and positioning. He also made a good save inside the box by being close to the ball and covering most angles. Lastly, in the 49th minute, he demonstrated his sharp reflexes again by promptly rushing out to stop the attacker from shooting/crossing inside the box, which led to a goal kick.

All in all, Georgsson had again a decent performance on round 2, demonstrating his very good ability on and off the ball. He needs to work a bit on his stance while rushing out following that first goal conceded, and he needs to remember that communication is also key.

Match_Summary_Notes:
In another dominant game for KR, Georgsson played well, despite the 2 goals he conceded. He knew how to sort himself out in nearly every situation against an opponent that pressed more aggressively than the first 2 rounds.
- In Possession: Georgsson had his highest passing accuracy yet with 89% of his passes being completed. He knew how to make good passes in nearly every situation, even though IBV were using a man-to-man pressing system and were more aggressive than Keflavík and Þór. He was very composed overall and was not scared to play through the middle, bypassing the first line of pressure, which helped with ball progression. As for his long balls, e could have done better. He had good ideas but overall the execution was poor, leading him to a 38% accuracy. Concerning the first goal he conceded, he made a big error as he gave the ball away, under less pressure than other situations he was in during the game. He took too much time to decide who he wanted to pass to and still chose the wrong teammate. These errors decrease with experience, and with his great ability on the ball, there should not be cause for concern.
- Out of Possession: Georgsson did not have much to do during the game, as he had 1 high claim, 2 saves and 2 goals conceded. IBV failed to produce meaningful occasions on 2 of those counts, with a poor shot and a poor cross, so for 1 of the saves (his second) and the high claim, Goergsson did well in relatively easy situations. Moving on to the first save, Georgsson did very well to rush out and make himself bigger, as this helped him save a big occasion that IBV had at the start of the game. For his second goal conceded, he did well to track back after being so high, but when the attacker got close, he should have rushed out more than he did. He gave the attacker too much space, which led to a shot that was hard to save as he was a bit too far away from the attacker. He's shown better reflexes in other situations this season than when he conceded that goal. Overall, he had a rather positive performance off the ball despite the goals conceded, but looked a bit hesitant while rushing out sometimes.

In conclusion, Halldor had a decent performance yet again, even though he did concede 2 goals. He still had his best passing accuracy, and during 95% of the time, he showed his amazing ability on the ball and his great composure.

Match_Summary_Notes:
In a game where Georgsson was finally tested properly, he responded brilliantly with 3 good saves, 7/13 accurate long balls (highest count this season) and great maturity both on and off the ball, despite conceding 2 goals.
- In Possession: Georgsson showed amazing composure, making good use of his options. He faced a lot of pressure from the FH attackers but never wavered and made great short and long passes. He even created a goalscoring opportunity. As usual, he knew where to position himself to help his teammates under pressure, he knew how to drag in the attackers to liberate his teammates and pass them the ball, and applied the correct weight in most of his passes to make it easier for the receiver.
- Out of Possession: Georgsson did concede 2 goals again during that game, however, he did exactly what he should do. For the first goal, he did well to try and punsh the ball away especially while being under so much pressure, and for the second goal, he did well to try and cover the spaces in order to make it harder for the finisher. He just was unlucky the shots were so hard to save due to placement and how close the attackers were to the goal. Against FH's constant passes and runs in behind, Georgsson always chose correctly between whether (and when) to rush out, or whether to stay close to the goal, which let him cancel nearly all the attacking threat FH showed by either getting to the ball first or producing good saves like in the first minute, where he stayed on his toes and showed great reflexes. He also produced another nice save during the 6th minute (freekick) even though he could have done better with the handling.

All in all, Georgsson showed great maturity, and produced nice saves and passes to help his team win and progress the ball respectively. He was way sharper than against IBV and showed more confidence on the ball and while rushing out too.
This was his best performance of the season yet.

Match_Summary_Notes:
In another dominant win for the club, Georgsson had yet again a very positive performance overall, with fine saves and sharp reflexes.
- In Possession: Compared to the other games, Georgsson had a drop in performance on the ball. His short passing game was still very good (95% accuracy in passes inside his own half), whereas the same could not be said at all for his long passes. With 5 successful attempts in a total of 19 (26% accuracy), it looked like Georgsson was mainly booting the ball out of pressure without caring where it ends up, as long as it's far away from the goal and in the middle of the pitch, in opposite to the other games, especially the past one against FH where he had 7/12 successful long passes and even when he shot it out of pressure, he made sure to try and aim at one of his teammates. Nonetheless, his approach regarding the long balls against Valur is understandable with the pressure being put on him and the defenders during those instances, so there should be nothing to worry about especially since his short game was amazing as usual, calm and composed under pressure, picking the right option and not afraid of playing through the middle.
- Out of Possession: It's the second time in a row that Georgsson has been tested that much out of possession, as Valur produced many high quality attacking moments. Georgsson was up for it as he demonstrated very sharp situational awareness and reflexes, by knowing exactly when to rush out, when to stay on his line, and where to position himself. He produced a couple of big saves and as usual, his advanced positioning helped stop multiple attacks/counter-attacks for Valur and keep possession. As for the goal he conceded, while it is frustrating because of the context that surrounds it (being so close to a clean sheet, conceding in the last minute + conceding 2 goals a game before this game), there was nothing more Georgsson could personally do. It was a very hard situation as the attacker was 1 on 1 against him, had no pressure and acres of space in front of him. The defenders could be partially blamed too but in reality the assist was just a really good pass and the finish was great too. Georgsson tried to predict where it would go but he got it wrong which is okay, there was nothing else he could do.

In conclusion, Georgsson showed yet again that he's up for the task by having 2 very good saves, and demonstrating his sharp reflexes and awareness during various moments of the game, as well as his amazing technical ability to help ball progression. We are getting closer and closer to a clean sheet, it is definitely coming sooner than later, he just needs to keep on going like this.
Monthly_Analysis:
Halldor had a very demanding month of May, playing 6 games compared to 4 in April, and he continues to demonstrate his immense value to the team despite the lack of clean sheets. The sheer volume of his involvement this month highlights his stamina and his ability to remain a core piece of our structure.
- In Possession: Halldor was once again incredibly involved in the buildup phase. He saw his total passes jump from 265 in April to a massive 354 this month. Even with this increased workload and higher intensity from opponents, he remained composed under pressure and maintained a strong passing accuracy of 85.0%. However, there is a clear area for improvement regarding his long-range distribution. He attempted 26 long balls this month, a significant increase from just 8 last month, but his accuracy on these longer passes dropped drastically to 19.2%.
- Out of Possession: Halldor faced a lot of pressure this month and stepped up to make 22 total saves, improving his overall shot-stopping volume from April. He has shown good reflexes and an ability to make crucial saves on the line. He still is a bit hesitant during set-pieces about whether to rush out or stay on his line, so this is an area he has to improve on.

All in all, Georgsson had another positive month overall. He improved his stamina and proved he can handle a very high volume of in-possession actions while maintaining solid accuracy on short and medium passes.

Positive Points for May: [+] Short/Medium Passing   [+] Shot-Stopping
Areas to Improve for June: [-] Decision Making during Set Pieces   [-] Long Passes

Match_Summary_Notes:
The game against Stjarnan proved to be one of the most important wins of the season yet, after going 2-0 down in the first half, the team fought back really well to come back and win 2-3. Even though Georgsson conceded 2 goals yet again, he still played well.
- In Possession: As usual, Georgsson had a really good game on the ball, succeeding with 97% of his short passes. He made the correct pass mearly every time, picking the right teammate at the right time without putting them in danger of losing the ball. Despite Stjarnan's low intensity pressing, he provided good solutions and wasn't scared of piercing the first line of pressing. He remained composed the whole time. As for his long balls, he had a 33% success rate, with 4/12 long balls being accurate which could've been better. Most of the time the ideas were good, but the execution wasn't. He knew who he wanted to pass to but couldn't get it to his teammate very accurately, which isn't that easy to do.
- Out of Possession: Georgsson conceded 2 goals again, and the first one was avoidable. He was caught by surprise during the shot but he still could've done better to prevent it as it deflected off of him and went in. As for the second goal, he was just unfortunate, there was nothing he could've done. The scorer was lucky to have his first shot blocked by his own teammate to give him a second chance, or else it would've been a sure save by Georgsson. He still reacted well and was close to saving it. Despite conceding those 2 goals, he still produced a total of 5 saves with 4 of them being from shots that came inside the box. He demonstrated great reflexes and strength, mentally and physically, in order to produce those saves that turned out to be crucial, especially the 2 saves in the dying moments of the game. Other than that, he wasn't as involved as the other games since Stjarnan didn't use long balls that went towards him that often, especially because his positioning wasn't as advanced as usual. He still made 11 recoveries and defended his box well during set pieces. He knew when to rush out and when to stay on his line during those times which helped him preserve the lead.

All in all, Georgsson had a decent performance, making crucial saves and helping his team in progression as usual, even though he did concede 2 goals.

Match_Summary_Notes:
Georgsson had a decent performance against Fram, even though he still did concede 2 goals. It is not ideal at all, especially with 0 clean sheets in 7 league games this season, but there was still some good that came out of his performance.
- In Possession: Georgsson had another top-notch game when it comes to his short distribution as he remained composed and picked his targets and timing well, even though the opponent did not press as much as other teams. He still did well when he was pressed, despite the fact that he made an error in judgement and got lucky at around the 60th minute, when he was pressed and gave a pass that was a bit too weak. This should not be a cause for concern though as it is a very rare occurence this season. Concerning his long passes, Georgsson only had 2/8 that were successful (25%), a rate lower than usual, but unlike the game against Valur in round 5, his passes were targeted at specific players depending on the situation of the game, most of them came close to being successful, which shows a nice improvement.
 - Out of Possession: Georgsson conceded 2 goals but still delivered some great saves in dangerous situations. He was very quick to react and showed sharpness in difficult situations. He also mostly defended his box very well against physical opponents during set-pieces and looked quite comfortable in doing so. He knew when to rush out and when to stay on his line during the set-pieces. He also did well to rush out of his box to stop the passes in behind played by the deeper Fram players, even though most of the passes weren't good themselves. Regarding the first goal he conceded, he positioned himself exactly where needed but the shot was already very difficult to save, despite the fact that his dive lacked abit of power. As for the second goal, he got overpowered by 2 very physical players which made it very hard for him to defend his box. Afterwards, the ball fell in front of him and he should have taken the chance to catch it instead of trying to punch it away with his left hand, which he failed to do. Even though there was so much pressure on him in this specific moment, his decision-making was not the best and could have been way better, which could have saved the game.

In conclusion, Georgsson had another decent performance, especially when it comes to shot-stopping and distribution, but could have made better decisions regarding the second goal.

Match_Summary_Notes:
In a very poor collective display, where the team was slow to react both offensively and defensively, Georgsson was left to fight alone in defense and ended up conceding 6 goals.
- In Possession: Georgsson had a game without errors, showing once again his great technique by completing 91% of his passes, and most importantly, 50% of his long balls, one of his best performances on the ball this season. Despite this, his short passes didn't really prove to be effective as the opposition players simply let him have the ball and marked the deep receiving midfielders to halt any kind of progression. Moreover, even though Georgsson did complete 7/14 of his long balls, it seemed like he was really hesitant to play this kind of pass many times and would have preferred simple, short passes to not risk anything. This is an area to improve as he could have offered a great solution to the attackers considering they were better than the Breidabik players aerially. He should have tried more of those passes because he could have created chaos in their defensive areas, giving an advantage to our attackers.
- Out of Possession: Georgsson conceded 6 goals that game, but the defenders did not really help him out that game as they did not track the Breidabik players properly leaving them with easy shots on goal many times. That being said, Georgsson could have done better on some occasions, like the second and fifth goals where he could have reacted faster and better, even though the box was so crowded. This is another area to improve, especially taking into account the deflections that could happen in these situations. He still produced 3 big saves in tough situations, highlighting his good shot-stopping ability and reflexes.
In summary, even though the team did not play well collectively and conceded 6 goals, Georgsson still got some positives out of this game with his long balls and shot-stopping ability. He did show good reflexes and did make some good long passes, but as mentioned earlier, while they are situations that are hard to control in game, Georgsson still has some improvements to make in these aspects.

Against Valur, the entire team showed a great mentality by bouncing back from the big defeat against Breidablik to win this game 3-1, and Halldor had a positive contribution to that even though he conceded a goal.
- In Possession: Halldor was very confident in his short passes like always. He nearly always picked the correct option to help evade the opposition’s pressing. Even though he had a passing accuracy of 78%, a drop compared to most of his other games, he still proved himself to be reliable on the ball. However, he did attempt more long balls than other games, with 17, for a success rate of 29% (5 in total). This increase was due to the fact that the defenders and midfielders close to him were being man-marked sometimes and could not offer a real solution. He appeared more confident in playing those long balls, and even with this success rate, the ideas were good as he wanted to progress play in another way and did not randomly boot the ball. Despite this, there were some instances where he could have done better. In fact, his hesitation to play long balls and preference for short passes was still visible at times, as the opposition players recognised that and started pressing more intensively. This led to him nearly losing the ball once and nearly letting one of the receivers be cornered by the opponent another time.
- Out of Possession: Without the ball, Halldor played really well against Valur, as he stopped multiple long balls by rushing out of his box to claim it and nullify the threat, defended his box well during set pieces against a physical team, and most importantly, pulled off a magnificent save from point blank range. These actions highlighted his sharp reflexes and shot-stopping ability that are improving game by game. Even though he did concede a goal through penalty, which he was unlucky to concede, Halldor had a positive impact on the game through his out of possession actions.

Finally, Halldor had a good performance against Valur, deserving of a clean sheet, as he showcased yet again his great technical ability, and swift reflexes and shot-stopping ability.

Against Akureyri, Halldor mostly had a good game being as reliable as ever, despite his great performance on the ball being ruined by the 3 goals conceded during the second half.
- In Possession: Halldor had a near flawless performance on the ball, completing 92% of his passes even though he received the ball a bit less than usual with 36/39 passes completed. He played good, precise passes and his short passes were perfect. He knew who to play them to, liberating his teammates from pressure by sometimes breaking the first pressing line, played at a high pace when required to do so and most importantly was very confident during the in possession phase of the game. He was reliable, and even though Akureyri's pressing wasn't as intense as some of the other teams faced, he still picked his passes really well and showed a lot of confidence in his touches and passes.
- Out of Possession: Halldor pulled off some great saves that game, including 2 very big saves, one in each half to keep his team in the lead. With these saves, he demonstrated again his great reach and reflexes which were crucial. However, he still conceded 3 goals, despite the first 2 not being his fault. He did everything he could for the first one and was unfortunate that the ball came back directly to Akureyri's #29 to score in an open net. For the second goal, he was caught by surprise with a very well placed shot which was very hard to save. He could have tried to dive but given the ball's trajectory and speed it would have been a very hard save to make anyway. Lastly, for the third goal, Halldor made an error in judgement during the corner as he initially tried to rush out to catch/punch the ball and then decided to go back on his line. This affected his positioning as Akureyri's third scorer had an easy header on the back post which Halldor was too slow to reach. The situation would have been different had he fully committed to one of the available options (staying on his line and making the save, giving him more time to position himself correctly or rushing out and catching the ball which was possible given that the center of the box was not as crowded as against other teams during set pieces). Halldor also showed good thinking by rushing out when Akureyri played long balls or passes in behind. This let him nullify any kind of danger coming from these passes and let the team regain possession.

In conclusion, Halldor had a decent performance against Akureyri by producing some great saves and having a near-perfect game in possession, but he still needed to do better duringthe goals conceded as especially the third one could have been avoided.

Positive Points:
[+] Play Through
[+] Shot Stopping

Area to Improve:
[-] Decision Making during Set Pieces

In KR's defeat away at Vikingur, Halldor conceded 2 goals and had a pretty average outing, with a not so impacting performance on the ball and not much to do off the ball excluding both goals conceded.
- In Possession: Halldor did not have a great performance in possession with a 77% passing accuracy (37/48), most of those passes not being able to have a big impact on the build-up. While he tried his best to look for good options nearby, Vikingur's intensive pressing made it difficult for him and oftentimes the lack of receivers in the middle did not help when there was a lot of space to be exploited there and a possibility to break the first line of pressing and progress the attack. While he looked more confident delivering long balls, his accuracy was not good either, with 3/13 long balls being successful (23%). Even though this is not really an easy thing to improve because of the distance between the goalkeeper and the receiver during these situations, both players in these situations could do better (Halldor and the receiver), by having Halldor trying to be more precise and the receiver being more proactive when the ball is in the air.
- Out of Possession: During that game, other than the 2 goals conceded, Halldor did not really have anything to do with Vikingur having only 4 shots on target, 2 of them being saved. The saves were good but easy to make considering the placement of the shot and the relatively low speed of the ball but it is still a positive thing for Halldor as he can build confidence on this. Now for the first goal conceded, the shot came from a corner. The situation was a bit chaotic with the number of people inside the box and Halldor's field of vision being therefore a bit blocked, but given the good position he took to prepare for the shot, he was not quick enough to dive in order to save the shot. This is a goal he could have prevented had he taken less time to prepare himself to dive. As for the second goal which came after a defensive error, he did well to rush out and try to block a possible shot as well as the passing lane but ultimately there was nothing else he could really do in this situation.

All in all, Halldor was not that bad against Vikingur but he definitely had better performances. It was an average performance from him as he mostly did what he could in most situations but could have still done better, so maybe it was a 5/10.

Poitive Point:
[+] Confidence in long balls

Areas to Improve:
[-] Precision during long balls
[-] Sharpness during crowded/chaotic situations

The team showed great mentality and resilience against IA to fight back everytime after conceding in order to win 5-3, but Halldor still conceded 3 goals and the first clean sheet of the season in the league is still not here. Even though Halldor did concede 3 goals, he still played well.
- In Possession: On the ball, he was reliable as always, with 41/54 accurate passes, or a 76% passing accuracy. He showed his great technical ability and stayed calm and composed under pressure, even though it was not as intense as the pressure he faced in some other games. He still was very reliable when he had to make short passes, finding solutions quickly when needed and delivering accurate passes. As for his long balls, there is a small improvement from last week with 5/17 accurate long balls, or an accuracy of 29%, so a 6% increase, which could be better. Against IA, even though he did try to pass it forward instead of kicking it out, it seems he sometimes did not find a good player to pass it to which partly explains the 12 inaccurate balls, and other times, he aimed well but his teammates could not win the aerial duels because they were often slow to react.
- Out of Possession: Despite the 3 goals conceded, Halldor had a great performance off the ball with 3 saves from inside the box that were crucial to let the team win. He showed great sharpness and reflexes by staying on his toes all the time when the ball was close and was quick to react on loose balls in order to claim them and nullify any potential danger. Now moving on to the goals conceded. For the first goal, despite the attacker being alone and so close to the goal, Halldor could have done better by closing down the space between him and his near post, as well as being a bit quicker to react there. The second goal was preventable too with better communication between him and Gabriel, even though Gabriel should have paid more attention. As for the third goal, it was something very hard to prevent from Halldor's end considering all the commotion inside the box, but being a little bit closer to the near post or running a bit sooner would have helped, but with all the chaos and the bodies blocking him it was still very hard to move quickly.

In conclusion, Halldor had a decent performance especially with the saves he pulled off, but there is room for improvement as he is still on the hunt for his first clean sheet of the season.

Positive Points:
[+] Shot stopping
[+] In possession intelligence for short passes

Areas to Improve:
[-] Sharpness/reflexes inside the box for close shots
[-] Scanning before going long

In the 3-2 defeat against Keflavik, Halldor had a rather bad performance, with 2 of the 3 goals being preventable from his end.
- In Possession: On the ball, he stayed as reliable as usual with 30/40 successful passes (75% accuracy rate), most of them being short passes. To give some context, most of these short passes were inconsequential with the other players being too static in possession while waiting for Halldor's passes, and being man-marked too, so his only options were to pass to the CBs, who faced the same problems as him. He made 30 passes in his own half, 28 of them being successful. This means that he made 10 passes in the opposition half, and only 2 of them were successful, which proves that Keflavik's strategy worked to halt progression through Halldor directly, one of our main assets on the ball. As for his long balls, he had a success rate of 25% (3/12), which has become a pretty regular rate. As usual, the ideas are there, he mostly knows where he wants to pass and can get the ball near that area most of the time (unless it is a clearance quickly made under pressure), but at the same time it is either a little bit too inaccurate or the receiver gets beaten in the air.
- Out of Possession: Off the ball, excluding the goals conceded, Halldor was not actually that bad (but not that good either, maybe a 5 or 6/10). He pulled off a great save and knew exactly when to rush out, which demonstrated his sharpness and good reflexes at times, but that is it. It is also very hard to ignore the 3 goals as they are a major part of the context and obviously the reason for the loss, so they ruined his performance which was not that good anyway, especially since 2 of those were avoidable. For the first goal which was a header that came after a direct cross, even though the attacker managed to lose his marker and get free for the header, Halldor seems to have reacted too early without taking into consideration the possibility of a header happening. For the second goal, his positioning after the ball was kicked from the corner was awful as he hid behind the defender, blocking his own field of vision. In consequence, he conceded a flick that would have been easily savable. As for the third goal, the shot was very hard to save and he did all he can to try and save it but the placement was just too good.

In conclusion, Halldor had one of his worst performances of the season if not the worst while taking the whole context into account with 2 preventable goals conceded. He should have demonstrated better reflexes inside the box and better composure during set pieces.

Positive Points:
[+] Passing
[+] Timing when rushing out

Areas to Improve:
[-] Reflexes and composure during set pieces
[-] Positioning during set pieces
[-] Timing of reactions during crosses
